title: Memory-mapped pytorch-pickle loader
description:
Spun out from #609 which landed mmap GGUF. The pytorch-pickle loader (`ferrotorch-serialize/src/pytorch_import.rs::load_pytorch_state_dict`) reads the whole zip archive into a heap Vec via `std::fs::read`, then does pickle parsing. Mmap'ing is more involved than GGUF because the pickle parser wants `Read` on a sub-file rather than a borrowed slice — but at minimum we should mmap the outer zip file. A shallower win: feed the mmap'd bytes through `Cursor` instead of allocating a Vec.

Same safety contract as #587 / #609 — mmap dropped before loader returns, no borrows escape.
